purpose: Adds a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return -ENODEV when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing commands from being queued after the device is being unregistered, which could cause a use‑after‑free.
Removes the unnecessary #include <asm/termios.h> from drivers/tty/n_hdlc.c because linux/tty.h already pulls in the required termios definitions, simplifying the file's includes.
Adds the 'N/A' store metric to the perf c2c documentation, explaining its meaning for store reference and store refs and updating the report table description.
Fixes race and lock handling in hugetlb VMA operations, allocating a new vma lock on split, adjusting unmap handling, and ensuring lock presence for shared mappings.
Adds AVDD-supply and MICVDD-supply properties to the rt5682s device‑tree binding YAML, documenting required regulators and updating the required list and example.
Adds missing iounmap() in zorro7xx_remove_one() to release I/O memory mapping allocated during probe, fixing a resource leak.
Removes the unused 'work' field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the Mellanox mlx5e kTLS TX driver; no functional change, just dead code cleanup.
Changes cfg80211_auth_request.key_idx from unsigned u8 to signed s8 so that a sentinel value -1 can be used when the key pointer is NULL, eliminating static analysis warnings.

The commit reorders the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS entry in two RISC‑V defconfig files to match the ordering produced by savedefconfig, removing the original line and thereby only affecting file layout without changing kernel behaviour.
Adds inline helper mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() and replaces direct ctrlmode checks with calls to it, improving code readability and maintainability.
Adds checks to selftest RTC code to skip tests when the RTC device file is absent, preventing false test failures on platforms without RTC.

The patch removes an unnecessary assignment to the variable 'bytes' in snd_echo_midi_output_write, leaving only 'sent = 0'. The variable was overwritten later, so the initial store was dead code, causing a clang static analysis warning.

The patch makes rcuscale independent of the RCU Tasks subsystem by conditionally compiling the tasks_ops code and removing the Kconfig dependency, updating test configs accordingly, thus avoiding unnecessary inclusion of RCU Tasks in PREEMPT_NONE kernels.
The patch corrects a duplicated phrase in a comment within spte.h, changing "be split be split" to "be split" without altering code logic.

Reverts a recent change that populated the DCN315 clock table using DCFCLK values, because when DCFCLK states are disabled the firmware provides empty entries, leading to underflow. Restores previous logic that avoids using those values.
Adds the Renesas RZ/V2L (r9a07g054) Mali‑G31 GPU compatible string to the arm,mali-bifrost devicetree binding documentation and updates the schema to accept it.
Renames the DesignWare PCIe port structure from pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across the driver, updating all declarations, definitions and function signatures for naming consistency.
Changes the printk format specifier in xgbe driver from %hx to %x to match the promoted int type, eliminating a clang -Wformat warning.
The patch casts the upper bound of the branch offset check to long, ensuring a signed comparison and preventing false WARN_ON triggers for negative offsets in MIPS jump label handling.

The patch removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU(), calls rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd() directly in the callers, and deletes the corresponding enum entry, simplifying the driver code.

Adds the static qualifier to the cavium_erratum_23154_cpus array in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c, fixing sparse warnings about the symbol not being declared static and limiting its linkage to the file.
The commit updates two devicetree binding YAML files, removing interrupt flag cells from MDSS consumer nodes because the provider supplies a single cell, and adds a label to the MDSS node.
Adds allocation support for array controls in v4l2_ctrl_ref, renames dynamic‑array fields to generic array fields, and updates related logic to enable drivers to change array sizes.
Adds entries for new Qualcomm SoCs and PMIC models to the socinfo driver by extending static lookup tables.

The patch modifies rtl8xxxu_print_chipinfo to compute the chip cut character directly (using 'A' + chip_cut for values 0‑15) and removes the previous switch that printed "unknown" for cuts beyond E, improving the logging of chip version.

Removes the redundant is_enabled field and its associated enable/disable/is_enabled functions from the ux500 PRCMU clock driver, relying on the core's existing enable count, and updates clk_ops tables accordingly.

The patch reorganizes XIP‑related conditional code in arch/riscv/mm/init.c, moving extern declarations and macro definitions, grouping them to minimise #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage, without altering runtime behaviour.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA nodes in the sm6350 device tree by setting their status to "okay", allowing I2C peripherals to be functional on the Sony Xperia Lena platform.
The patch updates comments in rzg2l_thermal.c: replaces an unclear comment with a clearer one, fixes a typo (caliberation → calibration), and adjusts comment formatting to match kernel coding style.

The patch changes igc_release_swfw_sync_i225 to stop spinning on a hardware semaphore. If the semaphore cannot be obtained (e.g., hardware surprise‑removed), it now logs an error and returns instead of looping forever.
Renames driver variables and logic to use the newer ASoC DAI clock provider/consumer terminology, updating format masks and error messages accordingly, without altering functionality.
The patch deletes a conditional definition of IS_SWAPFILE in fs/nfs/file.c because the macro is already always defined elsewhere, making the code dead and unnecessary.

Implements kernel‑side handling of GTP Echo Request/Response for GTPv0 and GTPv1-U, adding restart counter support and netlink attributes, so devices created without a userspace daemon comply with the GTP specification.
Replaces cpumask_next* calls with cpumask_first* where start is -1, adds cpumask_first_zero helper, and updates several drivers and core files accordingly, improving code clarity and efficiency.

The patch removes the driver’s attempt to read TIM information via the "dtim_assoc" IOVAR after a roam. Firmware no longer supports this call, causing repeated error logs. By eliminating the query, the driver stops generating the failure messages.
Adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for Intel SOF platforms to support the alc5682 codec without a speaker amplifier, and registers it in the ACPI machine match table.
Adds a guard in io_sendmsg_zc to reject zero‑copy sendmsg on sockets lacking the SOCK_SUPPORT_ZC flag, returning -EOPNOTSUPP.
Adds conditional loading of xeCJK.sty in the documentation preamble, preventing LaTeX errors on systems where texlive-xecjk is absent.
The patch updates scripts/checkpatch.pl to ignore macro definitions that start with "case", preventing the tool from flagging such macros as errors.

The patch rewrites simpledrm to use the generic DRM atomic helper infrastructure (plane, crtc, encoder, connector) instead of the old simple‑KMS helpers, updating structures, init calls and helper functions without changing driver behavior.
Implements a parallel JPEG decode worker for MT8195, adding hardware selection, workqueue handling, and driver structures to run up to three decode engines concurrently.
Removes many VPRINTK debug prints and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb function from libata, as they are redundant with tracepoints, cleaning up the code.
Adds extended‑blank (extra vblank) support to allow zstate entry during FreeSync, introduces capability flags, debug option, new stream update fields, and programming of hardware registers.
Updates the hqd_destroy callback prototype to use enum kfd_preempt_type instead of uint32_t, aligning with actual implementations and eliminating a CFI type mismatch on AMD GPUs.
Updates the STM32 pinctrl devicetree binding YAML: renames property 'reset' to the correct 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to enforce schema, fixing a documentation typo.
Adds conditional compiler flags in the ath9k Makefile to suppress the -Warray-bounds warning produced by GCC 12 for mac.c, without changing driver functionality.
The patch updates the Allwinner sun8i-ss PRNG driver to use pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync, removing extra error handling and simplifying the code path.

Refactors io_uring fixed‑file handling: moves install logic to filetable, introduces internal install helper and a removal helper, changes APIs to accept io_ring_ctx instead of io_kiocb, and updates callers accordingly.
Splits the imx8mq VPU device tree binding into separate G1 and G2 decoder nodes, adds new compatible strings, deprecates the old one, and updates property constraints and examples.
The patch eliminates the unused IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and inlines its fields directly into IWL_DEVICE_BZ, reducing redundancy without altering runtime behavior.
